title: Practice
summary: Four sections: production line → substrate → operating rules → instrumentation evidence. Every claim grounds to a real file or repo path.

strategy.decision: Four sections in execution order — 01 production line (forge family lead with PUBLIC `forge` umbrella) → 02 substrate (hooks + skills/subagents + session mining + Hive coordination) → 03 operating rules (4 rules quoted verbatim from ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) → 04 instrumentation evidence (hesitation fold diagram + Poe corpus stats + adversarial test plan + GitHub-link footer per artifact).
strategy.why: DESIGN-PRINCIPLES.md §Stage 4 fact-check '/practice four sections' row locks this structure. The original 3-section brief conflated substrate with instrumentation and missed the agent/skill/Hive coordination layer entirely. Production-line section uses the schematic signature (ADR-0004) for the visual register.
strategy.shipped: v2 had no /practice page. The toolchain claim existed only as scattered references in /about and individual case-study copy — no catalog surface, no grounded artifacts.
strategy.gap: Proposed = standalone catalog page with 4 sections + grounded artifacts; Shipped = nothing.
strategy.question: Does the 4-section structure read as catalog-of-evidence or as essay-about-process? The brief's composition-order rule says slot-2 must be readout-bearing, not editorial.

currentState.annotation: New page introduced in v3. No proposed/shipped toggle needed — this slice has no shipped equivalent. Comparison view auto-omits per CONVENTIONS.md.
